what_it_does: This file defines the REGISTER_ELEMENT role: a Pydantic output schema (HowTo + RegisterElementOut) and a self-registering ROLE dict that prompt a model to turn one candidate mockup element, plus grounding context, into a proposed ui:// registry dossier — address, represents, howto, capabilities, maps_to_feature, and a discrete grounding tag — while explicitly refusing to auto-write the registry.
observations: Defines a nested HowTo Pydantic sub-model with what/what_you_can_do/how_to_change fields.
Defines RegisterElementOut with a closed-vocabulary Literal grounding tag: built|proposed|uncertain|defer.

The prompt_template enforces strict no-fiction rules: capabilities must come from a fixed five-word vocabulary, maps_to_feature must be a verbatim inventory id or the literal 'proposed', and addresses must nest under the parent.
Includes a 'defer' rule: if a selector already maps to a registered ui:// the role must not mint a duplicate address.
input_addresses is declared as ('utterance','ground','exemplars','inventory'), with a comment explaining a recent change from a templated run:// address to a bare-name 'ground' resolved via unit_ctx.
The 'context' field is described as a declarative forward contract that is not currently auto-injected into the prompt by run_role.
mode_scope is set to {'registration'} so the cast-beyond-listening seam can fire it.
The docstring is extensive and discusses engine-depth concerns: context efficiency, the run:// seam, the cascade (RG1-RG9), and mirroring screen_reader.py.
No runtime logic is present; enforcement is entirely via prompt instructions and the output schema.
standouts: Grounding is expressed as a discrete Literal tag rather than a numeric confidence, routing operator triage.
Strong anti-hallucination constraints: closed capability vocabulary, verbatim feature ids only, explicit 'proposed' fallback.
Nested HowTo object instead of flat scalar fields, matching the shape of existing registry entries.
Explicit 'defer' behavior to avoid duplicate address proposals.
Propose-only floor: the role never writes the registry; operator approval is required downstream.
conventions: Follows the Pydantic BaseModel + self-registering ROLE dict pattern seen in other role files.
Uses output_schema to declare the model response shape.
Uses input_addresses to declare extra grounding inputs resolved by the runtime.
Uses mode_scope to mark the role as fireable in a specific cast.
Mirrors the structure of screen_reader.py (Pydantic class + module-level ROLE dict).
Heavy inline documentation explaining the engine contract and known limitations.
concerns: The prompt_template is very long and mixes rules, examples, and output schema instructions, which may be brittle to maintain.
The 'context' field openly admits it is not currently auto-resolved into the prompt, creating a forward contract that could drift from actual runtime behavior.
No code-level validation enforces the closed vocabulary or parent-nested addressing; compliance depends entirely on the model following the prompt.
The input_addresses declaration changed semantics based on a cascade resolution mechanism that is not visible in this file.
default_model and default_base_url are None, so callers must supply model configuration.
notes: A schema-and-prompt role that proposes registry dossiers for mockup elements under strict no-fiction, closed-vocabulary, and operator-gated rules.
questions: What is the exact schema of the candidate element placed at ctx['utterance'] by RG1?
How does the runtime resolve the bare-name 'ground' input per unit via unit_ctx?
Who populates 'exemplars' and 'inventory' in the shared ctx, and what are the actual 58 feature ids?
How is 'selector already maps to a registered ui://' detected before the role fires?
What does the operator approval step (RG8) look like, and how are proposed dossiers written back (RG9)?
Is the role-spec 'context' field planned to be auto-injected in a future engine version, or will it remain declarative?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it defines a propose-only registration role with a structured output schema and grounding rules. The extensive docstring goes beyond the code, but the code itself is consistent with that purpose.
apparent_themes: Context efficiency and engine contract documentation
intention_signals: The file is trying to prevent a language model from corrupting a design registry by inventing capabilities or feature ids, by constraining outputs to a known inventory, nesting addresses under known parents, and surfacing uncertainty through discrete routing tags rather than numeric confidence scores.
novelty: The registration step is framed as a propose-only, operator-gated map operation with an explicit 'defer' state for already-registered selectors and a discrete no-confidence grounding tag; it also documents the engine-level 'seam' of how grounding context is actually delivered through input_addresses rather than the role-spec context field.
contribution: It contributes a structured registry-dossier schema (nested HowTo + RegisterElementOut) and a prompt contract that enforces closed-world capability and feature vocabulary, parent-nested addressing, and a discrete grounding/defer tag to protect registry integrity during generation.
summary_for_embedding: roles/register_element.py defines the REGISTER_ELEMENT role with Pydantic output schemas and a self-registering ROLE dict that maps one grounded mockup element to a proposed ui:// registry dossier under strict no-fiction, closed-vocabulary, and operator-gated rules.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to give the registry-generation chain a propose-only map step that emits structured, grounded dossiers without auto-writing the registry or hallucinating feature ids.

symbols.does: Defines the nested howto sub-model with what, what_you_can_do, and how_to_change string fields.
symbols.description: HowTo is a Pydantic BaseModel that captures the three-part voice used by existing registry entries, representing what an element is, what an operator can do with it, and how it is changed, expressed at non-developer altitude.

symbols.does: Defines the top-level output schema for a proposed registry dossier.
symbols.description: RegisterElementOut is a Pydantic BaseModel that declares the proposed ui:// address, a short represents phrase, a nested HowTo object, a capabilities list, a maps_to_feature field that must be a real inventory id or 'proposed', and a discrete Literal grounding tag (built/proposed/uncertain/defer).
